name: mitotic cell cycle
description: "Progression through the phases of the mitotic cell cycle, the most common eukaryotic cell cycle, which canonically comprises four successive phases called G1, S, G2, and M and includes replication of the genome and the subsequent segregation of chromosomes into daughter cells. In some variant cell cycles nuclear replication or nuclear division may not be followed by cell division, or G1 and G2 phases may be absent." [GOC:mah, ISBN:0815316194]

description: "A non-membranous macromolecular complex containing proteins and translationally silenced mRNAs. Different types of RNA granules (RGs) exist, depending on the cell type and cellular conditions." [GOC:go_curators, GOC:sp, PMID:16520386, PMID:20368989, PMID:21436445]
